latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

BYRGPD6
TIDM: ALF

As at the close of business on 30 June 2025, the final Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.1569
151%
As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.0365
154% - see Note
Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.
An analysis of the portfolio as at June 30 and September 30 2025 is provided in the quarterly factsheet which is now available on the Company's website, please see the link below.
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/fact-sheets-and-fund-updates/2025/
Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group is a small UK‑listed company that buys, manages and sells commercial property assets, mainly retail and office space, and earns rental income from its portfolio.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The group operates in a niche that serves mid‑size tenants who need flexible, well‑located spaces. Its portfolio is diversified across a handful of high‑traffic sites, giving it a modest competitive edge in a market that still values physical storefronts.
company_30s.current_state: The company has recently returned capital to shareholders and is focused on tightening its balance sheet. It is trading at a very low price, which suggests the market views it as a distressed or undervalued asset.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show a steady stream of shareholder‑friendly actions: a return of capital, updates on holdings, and a new factsheet outlining the net asset value. These moves signal that the company is trying to improve liquidity and give investors a clearer picture of its underlying value.
A second theme is stability: no new acquisitions or debt issuances have been announced, indicating the company is maintaining its existing portfolio rather than chasing growth.
The third theme is communication: the company has been keeping its investors informed through regular updates, which may help calm concerns about its small size and limited visibility.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the community is largely silent but a few voices see value in the low price.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the company’s low valuation relative to its asset base and the recent capital return as signs that the market is undervaluing the group.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the company’s thin trading volume and lack of growth initiatives could keep the price stagnant, and they fear the asset quality might not support the current valuation.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The sentiment has stayed flat; there’s no clear shift toward excitement or dread.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search activity, meaning the market is largely unaware of the company. This silence can work in our favor if a catalyst arrives.

near_term_catalysts: A potential catalyst could be a new asset acquisition announced in the next quarterly report.
If the company releases a positive earnings forecast or a higher dividend payout, that could spark buying interest.
A significant increase in trading volume, especially on a day the price breaks out of its low range, would be a strong technical trigger.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next 2–3 months are: a volume spike above the 20‑day average, a price break above the recent high of the last 30 days, and any mention of a new lease or partnership in the company’s press releases.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep trough – the price has fallen far below its historical average and there is no clear sign of institutional accumulation. If capitulation is complete, a modest catalyst could kick a mean‑reversion bounce.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the evidence does not yet support a clear contrarian bullish thesis.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company announces a new asset or improves its earnings outlook, the low price relative to its asset base could create a sizable upside. The mean‑reversion thesis would imply a recovery to roughly the middle of its historical range, potentially yielding a 30‑50% gain if the price climbs from its current level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s thin trading volume and lack of growth initiatives leave the risk of continued stagnation high. A further decline in the value of its property holdings or a failure to attract new tenants could push the price back down, with no obvious support level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: up to a 40% gain if the price rebounds to the midpoint of its 52‑week range. Potential downside: the price could fall further to the current level, representing a 100% loss of any investment made at that point. The asymmetry is modest and hinges on a single catalyst.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only if the price breaks above the last 30‑day high and volume exceeds the 20‑day average by 50%. Set a stop just below the recent low to limit losses, and target the midpoint of the 52‑week range for an exit.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a clear announcement of a new acquisition or a positive earnings revision. If the company releases a higher dividend or a share buyback plan, that could be a trigger.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals. The risk is primarily market risk rather than corporate failure.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With a silent market, a low price, and no clear catalyst, the stock is a waiting game: only a strong announcement or a technical breakout can justify a contrarian bet.
